codeReview.summary: The supposed fix does not implement the requested reopen-freshness hardening at all: the current code still caches maximize and focus actors forever by label, and the tests still only cover distinct-label isolation rather than same-label close/reopen refresh semantics.

issues.description: The maximize actor still captures Window.getCurrent() once at creation time and getWindowMaximizedActor() still reuses that actor indefinitely from a module-level map keyed only by label. A reopened window that reuses the same label will therefore inherit the stale actor bound to the disposed native window instead of creating fresh renderer-local ownership, so the original scrutiny failure remains unresolved.

The focus actor cache also remains a never-evicted module-level map keyed only by label, with no teardown or refresh path for close/reopen cycles. Even if browser focus listeners happen to be rebound on a fresh import, the implementation still does not encode the required same-label reopen freshness contract described by the fix feature.

The regression tests still only prove that different labels receive different actors. They do not simulate closing and reopening a window with the same label, do not assert that cached actors are torn down or recreated, and therefore still leave VAL-CONTROLS-007/VAL-CROSS-004 uncovered for the reported failure mode.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The mission library still documents module-scope window-handle caching as a pitfall but not the stronger same-label reopen rule now confirmed by two scrutiny passes: any per-window cache keyed by label must define disposal and recreation semantics for close/reopen cycles because Tauri labels are reused.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: .factory/library/windows.md mentions generic module-scope handle caching, while src/flow/windowMaximized.ts:48-76 and src/flow/windowFocus.ts:41-69 still keep never-evicted label maps that violate the reopen-freshness contract called out by both this review and the prior failed review.

summary: Fail: the current HEAD only changes the prior review JSON and leaves the underlying maximize/focus actor caches and tests unchanged, so the same-label close/reopen freshness bug is still present and the original blocking scrutiny issues are not addressed.
